title: When the River Came
subtitle: Who do you save when you can't save everyone?

turns.prompt: A record flood swallows a major region overnight. Sirens are wailing and the water is still rising.

choices.label: Order a full mandatory evacuation
choices.reaction: Lives first. Homes and businesses left to the water.

choices.label: Defend the city centre at all costs
choices.flavour: Save the engine of the economy.

delayed.headline: The poorer districts, left undefended, are devastated

choices.reaction: The downtown is saved. The outskirts drown.

choices.label: Mobilise communities to self-organise
choices.flavour: Trust people to save each other.
choices.reaction: Neighbours rescue neighbours. Coordination is chaos.

turns.prompt: The waters recede. The cameras leave. Now comes the rebuild.

choices.label: Rebuild with flood defences and green infrastructure
choices.reaction: Built to outlast the next storm. The bill is enormous.

choices.label: Rebuild fast and cheap to reopen the economy

delayed.headline: The next storm breaks straight through the cut-rate rebuild

choices.reaction: Doors reopen quickly. Corners were quietly cut.

choices.label: Abandon the floodplain and relocate residents
choices.flavour: Don't rebuild in the river's path.
choices.reaction: A hard, sensible retreat. Communities mourn their homes.

turns.prompt: Relief is slow, donors are wavering, and anger is boiling over in the camps.

choices.label: Borrow big for immediate relief
choices.reaction: Cheques and supplies flow. The deficit swells.

choices.label: Ration aid strictly to the worst-hit
choices.reaction: Funds stretched thin. Many feel abandoned.

choices.label: Lean on insurers and private rebuilding
choices.reaction: The insured rebuild fast. The uninsured wait in line.

turns.prompt: The shortcuts you took in the rush are now the disaster's second act.

memory_callbacks.text: The districts you left undefended are now the symbol of who you chose to save.
The cheap rebuild has failed at the very first test of weather.
The communities you relocated have not forgiven the loss of home.
The rationed aid left wounds that politics is now reopening.

choices.label: Create a permanent climate-resilience fund
choices.reaction: A shield for the future. A weight on the present.

choices.label: Cut losses and focus growth elsewhere
choices.reaction: The economy moves on. The region feels written off.

choices.label: Empower local mutual-aid networks
choices.flavour: Hand the recovery to those living it.
choices.reaction: Communities lead their own recovery, unevenly.

turns.prompt: The region rebuilds at last. What did the flood teach your country?

choices.label: A nation rebuilt to live with a changing climate
choices.reaction: Resilient and prepared. The cost shaped a generation.

choices.label: Prioritise growth and hope the storms hold off
Protect the centres, let the margins fend
choices.reaction: The core endures. The edges quietly erode.
